what_it_does: Implements the company-side edge for SHARED channels: it builds channel_posts rows, publishes them to Supabase via PostgREST as a least-privilege principal, and receives outbound Realtime INSERT events to inject posts into live company member sessions while skipping the originating session.
observations: Module is scoped only to SHARED channels; internal-only channels are explicitly left untouched.
Publishes directly to Supabase channel_posts and does not also inject locally, enforcing single-source-of-truth on Supabase.
Routing skips by origin session, not by sender_kind, so both company sessions and client/Claude Design rows inject.
Uses a dedicated least-privilege principal (COMPANY_CHANNEL_*) and RLS; service-role key is prohibited.
REST publisher returns structured ok/status/error results instead of raising, requiring callers to surface failures.
Realtime WebSocket transport is implemented but marked as stubbed/pending builder-provided topic, payload shape, and live verification.
Includes an __main__ offline self-test covering row building, publish ok/fail paths, routing, join message, parsing, and URL conversion.
Lazy-imports websocket so the publish path has no WebSocket dependency.
Heartbeat re-sends a fresh access_token to keep the RLS subscription alive past JWT expiry.
standouts: The phx_join payload carries the principal JWT as access_token; the docstring calls this load-bearing for RLS delivery.
route_inject deliberately rejects the earlier client-only filter, enabling company↔company visibility on shared channels.
publish_shared_post is designed to fail loud with a reason rather than silently dropping a shared post.
The Realtime subscriber is a daemon-threaded WebSocketApp with reconnect and token-refresh heartbeat, but live round-trip is explicitly not yet verified.
conventions: Dependency injection for I/O: poster, members_of, inject, on_status.
Pure functions for row construction and routing to enable offline unit tests.
Structured result dicts with ok/status/error instead of exceptions for expected failure modes.
Docstring-first design rationale embedded in the module header and per-symbol docstrings.
concerns: Realtime transport is stubbed/unverified; the live round-trip is the named gate.
Heartbeat interval is fixed and does not account for actual JWT expiry time.
on_insert swallows per-session inject exceptions, which is intentional but may mask broken sessions.
The shared-vs-internal channel discriminator and cc_channels hook are outside this file and unresolved.
Nested closure callbacks in start() capture state and could complicate testing/reasoning.
notes: This file is the additive shared-channel boundary: it writes outbound posts to Supabase and wires an RLS-gated Realtime subscriber to fan inbound posts into member sessions, with the live WebSocket handshake still pending builder details.
questions: What exact Realtime topic and postgres_changes payload shape will the builder specify?
How will the supervisor/cc_channels layer decide whether a channel is shared vs internal?
What is the full interface/behavior of SupabasePrincipal (url, anon_key, auth_headers, access_token)?
How are publish failures surfaced to the operator as a Notice and recorded as a Gap?
What does the 0005 RLS SELECT grant look like, and has it been applied to the live project?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it builds rows, publishes via authenticated REST, routes/injects by origin, and provides a Realtime subscriber scaffold exactly as described.
apparent_themes: Single-source-of-truth on Supabase for shared channels.
Additive boundary that does not disturb internal channel fabric.
Testable pure core with injected I/O.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a secure, auditable bridge between the company's internal live session fabric and a shared Supabase channel store, keeping internal channels untouched and ensuring company sessions and Claude Design share the same channel without mirroring.
novelty: Explicit design pivot away from a mirrored/client-only delivery model toward single-source Supabase delivery, with the skip-by-origin routing rule and load-bearing RLS access_token in the Realtime join both called out as correctness fixes.
contribution: A reusable, testable shared-channel boundary: row construction with validation, an authenticated fail-loud REST publisher, an origin-skipping injector router, and a WebSocket subscriber scaffold with RLS-aware join and token-refresh heartbeat.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/channel_boundary.py implements the company-side publish/read boundary for shared Supabase channels, including row building, authenticated PostgREST publishing, origin-skipping Realtime injection, and a daemon WebSocket subscriber scaffold.
intention_for_embedding: To let company sessions publish to and receive from a single-source Supabase shared channel while keeping internal channels and service-role credentials out of the path.

symbols.does: Constructs and validates a channel_posts row for a shared-channel post.
symbols.description: Defaults a thread id when none is provided, assembles the column map, and raises ValueError if any required field (channel_id, from_session, text) is missing so no unroutable row is published.

symbols.does: Publishes one shared-channel row to Supabase channel_posts via PostgREST.
symbols.description: Authenticates as the least-privilege principal using Bearer JWT headers, POSTs the row, and returns a structured result dict with ok, status, id, and error. Transport, HTTP, and auth failures are surfaced in the returned dict rather than raised, enforcing fail-loud behavior.

symbols.does: Decides which live member sessions should receive a new shared-channel post.
symbols.description: Returns all member sessions except the originating from_session, regardless of sender_kind, so both company-session and client/Claude Design rows inject into the shared channel.

symbols.does: Builds the Supabase Realtime WebSocket URL from the project HTTP URL and anon key.
symbols.description: Converts https to wss and http to ws, then appends the realtime/v1/websocket path with the apikey query parameter.

symbols.does: Builds the Phoenix phx_join frame for subscribing to postgres_changes INSERTs.
symbols.description: Includes the principal JWT as payload.access_token so that Supabase Realtime applies RLS to the subscription; without the token the subscription would silently deliver nothing.

symbols.does: Parses a raw Realtime WebSocket message into an event and optional record.
symbols.description: Returns the postgres_changes event and the INSERT record when present; for join acknowledgments, system messages, heartbeats, or non-JSON input it returns the event with None or (None, None).

symbols.does: Daemon-threaded outbound Realtime subscriber that injects shared-channel posts into live sessions.
symbols.description: Connects to Supabase Realtime, joins channel_posts INSERTs with an RLS-scoped access token, and fans each new row into company member sessions via an injected callback while skipping the origin.

symbols.does: Stores callbacks, principal, topic, table, and status sink for the subscriber.
symbols.description: Accepts members_of to resolve channel members, inject to deliver posts, a SupabasePrincipal for auth, the Realtime topic, the watched table, and an optional on_status logging callback.

symbols.does: Handles one new channel_posts row by routing and injecting it to member sessions.
symbols.description: Resolves the channel's live members, skips the originating session, calls inject for each target, logs per-target errors without aborting the fan-out, and returns the list of targeted handles.

symbols.does: Increments and returns the next Phoenix reference string.
symbols.description: Used to tag outbound join, heartbeat, and access_token frames with monotonically increasing ref values.

symbols.does: Starts the WebSocket connection and subscription in a daemon thread.
symbols.description: Lazily imports websocket, builds the join message with a fresh access token, opens a WebSocketApp with open/message/error/close callbacks, starts a heartbeat thread, and reconnects with backoff until stopped.

symbols.does: The daemon-thread loop that connects, subscribes, and reconnects.
symbols.description: Repeatedly builds a fresh join message and WebSocket URL, creates the WebSocketApp, runs it forever, and sleeps for backoff between reconnects unless reconnect is disabled or stop is set.

symbols.does: Sends the phx_join message when the WebSocket opens.
symbols.description: Transmits the pre-built join JSON and reports a 'joined' status for the topic.
Parses each raw message; if it contains a postgres_changes INSERT record, delegates to on_insert for fan-out delivery.

symbols.does: Reports WebSocket errors via the status sink.
symbols.description: Receives error objects from the WebSocketApp and forwards them as 'error' status events.

symbols.does: Reports WebSocket close via the status sink.
symbols.description: Triggered when the WebSocketApp connection closes, emitting a 'closed' status event for the topic.

symbols.does: Sends periodic Phoenix heartbeats and refreshed access tokens.
symbols.description: Every interval seconds it sends a heartbeat frame and an access_token frame with a freshly refreshed principal JWT, exiting when the socket closes or stop is set.

symbols.does: Signals the subscriber to stop and closes the WebSocket.
symbols.description: Sets the stop event and attempts to close the current WebSocketApp connection.
